command: Invoke-Command -ComputerName localhost -ScriptBlock { $log = "$env:SystemRoot\System32\LogFiles\Firewall\pfirewall.log"; Set-NetFirewallProfile -All -LogAllowed $true -LogDropped $true -LogFileName $log | Out-Null; Get-Content -Path $log -Tail 0 -Wait | ForEach-Object { if ($_ -match '\b(ALLOW|DROP)\s+UDP\s+(\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,3}){3})\s+\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,3}){3}\s+\d+\s+3544\b') { $src=$matches[2]; $name="teredo-rdp-$($src.Replace('.','-'))"; if (-not (Get-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $name -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $name -Direction Inbound -Action Allow -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 3389 -RemoteAddress $src -Profile Any | Out-Null; Start-Job { param($n) Start-Sleep -Seconds 600; Remove-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $n } -ArgumentList $name | Out-Null; } } } }
explanation: Treats inbound Teredo (UDP/3544) traffic as a signaling packet to open RDP (3389) for the source IP temporarily.
rationale_steps: Detect Teredo packets which are uncommon on many enterprise hosts
Create a scoped, temporary RDP allow rule for the source IP

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell is well-formed with balanced blocks and valid pipelines, but uses an invalid Set-NetFirewallProfile parameter (-LogDropped) that will cause a parameter-binding error.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: While it correctly implements a traffic-signaled trigger consistent with T1205, it is unlikely to achieve the stated behavior due to the invalid -LogDropped parameter, the need for administrative privileges to modify firewall settings, and dependence on WinRM for Invoke-Command to localhost.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed PowerShell with balanced braces and proper pipeline usage, though the double-escaped backslashes (\\) in the regex and path strings suggest the command was serialized/escaped for display rather than direct execution, which could cause minor parsing issues in some contexts.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements a port-knocking/traffic signaling mechanism (T1205) by monitoring Windows Firewall logs for Teredo UDP/3544 packets and dynamically creating temporary inbound firewall rules to allow RDP (TCP/3389) from the signaling source IP for 10 minutes, which is a realistic and operationally coherent implementation of the described technique.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command follows correct PowerShell syntax, including proper nesting of script blocks, regex escaping, and cmdlet usage.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command effectively implements host-based traffic signaling by monitoring firewall logs for a specific trigger (Teredo/3544) to dynamically and temporarily open an RDP port for the signaling source.
